title: JwtPayload
description: The structured payload of an inter-service HS256 JWT. Signed by identity-service via JwtSigner; verified locally by every downstream service via JwtVerifier. The `app` claim binds the token to a single Application; downstream services reject cross-Application-forged tokens with 403 JWT_APPLICATION_MISMATCH.

iss.description: Issuer. Always identity-service's public URL, e.g. `https://identity.stackra.com`. Downstream verifiers check this.

aud.description: Audience — the fully-qualified hosts the token is intended for. Downstream verifiers check their own hostname is in the array.

sub.description: Subject — the user_id (ULID `usr_...`) OR platform_user_id (ULID `plu_...`) OR service_account_id (ULID `svc_...`).

app.description: Application ID (ULID `app_...`). LOAD-BEARING — downstream services check `payload.app === config('app.slug')` before trusting any other claim.

tid.description: Tenant ID (ULID `tnt_...`). NULL for platform_admin subjects; NULL for service accounts scoped platform-wide.

sco.description: Scope claims — the caller's active organizational context resolved at token issue.

roles.description: Compact role name array (e.g. ['coach', 'staff']). Cached at issue-time from RBAC. Downstream services trust this for the token's lifetime.

permissions.description: Compact permission name array with scope suffixes (e.g. ['athletes.view.branch', 'sessions.create.own']). Cached at issue-time.

kid.description: Key ID matching an entry in the JWKS. Verifier uses this to select the correct signing key for signature verification.

jti.description: JWT ID. UUID. Consulted against auth_jwt_deny_list by downstream verifiers; matching entry = 403 JWT_DENIED.

purpose.description: Distinguishes the JWT's issuance context. Downstream services may branch on this (e.g. audit-log the caller kind differently).
